title: Python Data Visualization — From Code to Chart
description: Learn data visualization with Python. Nexora will explain the concepts, write code, and generate a chart — all live on screen.

tasks.title: Create a Python Chart Script
tasks.sonic_prompt: Now let me write a Python script that creates a bar chart. Watch the terminal as I type the code.

subtasks.title: Open a terminal
subtasks.sonic_prompt: First, I will open a terminal on the desktop.
subtasks.goal: Open the XFCE terminal application on the desktop.

subtasks.title: Create chart.py with matplotlib code
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will create a Python file with matplotlib code to generate a bar chart.
subtasks.goal:
In the terminal, run this exact command to create the chart script: cat > chart.py << 'EOF'
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

categories = ['Python', 'JavaScript', 'Go', 'Rust', 'Java']
popularity = [85, 78, 45, 40, 70]
colors = ['#3776ab', '#f7df1e', '#00add8', '#ce422b', '#f89820']

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(categories, popularity, color=colors)
plt.title('Programming Language Popularity 2025', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')
plt.ylabel('Popularity Score')
plt.xlabel('Language')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('chart.png', dpi=100)
print('Chart saved as chart.png')
EOF

subtasks.title: Run the script to generate the chart
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now let me run the script to generate our chart.
subtasks.goal: In the terminal, type: python3 chart.py and press Enter. The output should say 'Chart saved as chart.png'.

tasks.title: Open Jupyter Notebook and Explore Data
tasks.sonic_prompt: Now let me show you how to work with data in Jupyter Notebook using Pandas.

subtasks.title: Open Jupyter in Firefox
subtasks.sonic_prompt: I will open the Jupyter Notebook in the browser.
subtasks.goal: Open Firefox and navigate to http://localhost:8888. Jupyter Notebook should already be running.

subtasks.title: Create a new Python notebook
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will create a new Python notebook.
subtasks.goal: In the Jupyter file browser, click the 'New' dropdown button on the right side, then click 'Python 3' or 'Python 3 (ipykernel)' to create a new notebook.

subtasks.title: Write and run Pandas code
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Let me type some Pandas code to analyze data and display it.
subtasks.goal:
Click on the first empty code cell, then type: import pandas as pd
data = {'Language': ['Python', 'JavaScript', 'Go', 'Rust', 'Java'], 'Score': [85, 78, 45, 40, 70]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df.to_string(index=False))
Then press Shift+Enter to run the cell. The output should display a formatted table.

tasks.title: View the Generated Chart
tasks.sonic_prompt: Finally, let me open the chart image we generated earlier so you can see the result.

subtasks.title: Open the chart image
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Let me open the chart we created.
subtasks.goal: In the terminal, type: xdg-open chart.png and press Enter. This should open the chart image in an image viewer.
